title: Net declarations using built-in types should be limited to 4-state data types
body:
Icarus Verilog currently accepts the following net declarations:
````
wire bit [7:0] b;
wire real r;
````
This is illegal according to IEEE 1800-2017 section 6.7.1 which states that a net must be composed entirely of 4-state bits.

I've tested some other simulators and they all rejected these declarations.

If we want to keep these as Icarus extensions, the 2-state case needs some further work to prevent it taking on undefined values when multiply driven.
code_blocks:
wire bit [7:0] b;
wire real r;
